title: blazewrit harness — investigate step structured_output
description: STEP 3 INVESTIGATE structured_output schema (M1 = SHAPE, grammar-constrained). Investigate interprets Ground FACTS into a designable problem definition: impact_map, constraints, risk_surface, architecture_impact (R6 force INPUTS Decide reads), a 5-state compatibility_verdict, and the 7-disposition ground_unknowns_addressed matrix. It does NOT generate options or make decisions (Decide) and does NOT capture new project-internal facts (Ground). TARGET RUNTIME: grammar-constrained generation — the model cannot emit a wrong shape/enum/missing-required field. Fields needing TRUTH (counts↔raw_stdout, every cited line exists, derivation consistency, declared_next_step==expected_next_step) are closed by SIBLING M2 validator_contracts listed in `x-validator-manifest` below — NOT by any keyword here. §5: every cross-step ref is a Postgres RowRef, never a file path (legacy '.blazewrit/grounds/**' / 'flow-history/<id>.json' is PRE-§5 and translated). M3: ED/MCP-backed and external-research-backed fields that can be ABSENT/FAIL/timeout use DegradableMeasurement (Measured|Omitted), never null/sentinel/placeholder-string. R23: every measured count is CountClaim. R26: every fact/risk/issue/disposition item carries source_tool provenance. R13: unverified flags KEEP-persist (no 'no unverified remain' forbid).

x-validator-manifest.$comment: M2 deterministic code checks the orchestrator MUST run AFTER grammar produces a shape-valid object. These are NOT schema keywords — grammar cannot enforce truth/cross-field/cross-run consistency. Listed here as the SIBLING contracts for this step's truth-bearing fields.

validator_contracts.rule: R23/R32 — impact_map.affected_files_count.value MUST equal impact_map.affected_files.length (mechanical derive, dedup'd). Investigate has NO Bash, so the count is NOT a CountClaim (no command/raw_stdout): re-derive value from the affected_files_count.derived_from upstream rows (based_on_ground row's task_subgraph.entry_nodes + this investigation's impact_map.ripple), NOT from any re-hashed stdout.
validator_contracts.check: assert affected_files_count.value == dedup(affected_files).length AND value re-derivable from affected_files_count.derived_from rows (resolve each RowRef, union entry_nodes file paths + ripple file-type node paths, dedup, count)

validator_contracts.rule: Derivation rule (README L77-81) — affected_files = union(Ground.task_subgraph.entry_nodes file paths, impact_map.ripple file-type nodes), dedup'd. If entry_nodes unchanged but an affected file is missing, FAIL (investigate-reviewer parity).
validator_contracts.check: re-derive affected_files from based_on_ground row's task_subgraph.entry_nodes + ripple; assert equality (set)

validator_contracts.rule: R6 force-trigger derive — has_architecture_level == (new_modules.length>0) OR (public_api_changes.length>0); orchestrator computes it (Investigate does not free-fill). public_api_changes == external_surface[].contract where breaking==true.
validator_contracts.check: assert has_architecture_level == (new_modules.length>0 || public_api_changes.length>0) AND public_api_changes == {c.contract : c in external_surface, c.breaking==true}

validator_contracts.rule: compatibility-verdict.md V1-V14b — V1 (empty issues AND entry_nodes nonempty)=>proceed; V2 splits on scope when any blocks_flow=true: V2a (any blocking issue project-wide OR blocking scope-union covers all affected area => blocked, all blocking issues scope_confined=false); V2b (all blocking issues scope-confined + proceed remainder>=1 => not blocked, defer to V13 partial_proceed, those issues scope_confined=true); V3 (no V2a) any requires_user=>needs_clarification; V4 else proceed; V5 dedup (root_cause hash+scope hash, max severity); V6 issues<=50 else issues_overflow; V7 type=other=>custom_type; V8 blocked=>blockers nonempty; V9 needs_clarification=>open_questions nonempty; V10 every issue has evidence; V11 no_op=>no_op_details complete; V12 result enum valid; V13 partial_proceed iff V2b (partial_scope_handling required); V14 (Compound) — flow_type=='compound' REQUIRES sub_flow_verdicts PRESENT and non-empty (for ANY result, not just no_op; so the aggregate-no_op precondition can never be vacuously satisfied by omission), AND result=no_op iff sub_flow_verdicts all result=no_op; V14b (Compound mixed) some no_op + some non-no_op => result decided by non-no_op sub-flows, no_op sub-flows preserved in partial_scope_handling.no_op_set (partial_proceed only) or in sub_flow_verdicts[result=no_op] (proceed/blocked/needs_clarification — those branches forbid partial_scope_handling, and sub_flow_verdicts is verdict-level optional permitted in every branch), never blocked_set/proceed_set. COMPOUND PRESENCE (sub_flow_identification + sub_flow_verdicts): flow_type=='compound' REQUIRES both sub_flow_identification (non-empty) and sub_flow_verdicts (non-empty) present; flow_type!='compound' REQUIRES both absent.
validator_contracts.check: run V1..V14b over compatibility_verdict.issues and result branch; assert V2a/V2b scope split via issue.scope_confined (V2a all blocking scope_confined=false flow-wide; V2b all blocking scope_confined=true + proceed remainder>=1 -> partial_proceed); V14/V14b (Compound-conditional) — flow_type is NOT a field of this schema (it lives upstream in Triage, resolved via based_on.triage_ref), so sub_flow_verdicts/sub_flow_identification stay unconditionally optional in grammar and their Compound-conditional presence is M2-only here: when based_on.triage_ref's flow_type=='compound' assert sub_flow_identification present+non-empty AND sub_flow_verdicts present+non-empty, then assert V14/V14b sub_flow_verdicts aggregation and no_op_set routing; when flow_type!='compound' assert both sub_flow_identification and sub_flow_verdicts absent; reject on any violation grammar cannot see

validator_contracts.rule: R26 provenance — every issue.evidence, no_op_details.evidence, risk item evidence, unknown_ref, based_on_ground, and based_on.* MUST resolve to an existing Postgres step_runs / sub-row id (RowRef), not a dangling/file ref. EXEMPT: ground_unknowns_addressed[].follow_up_ref (and its sub_dispositions[] recursion) is NOT a RowRef — it is a LOCAL invocation-scoped id pointing at an in-document collection item (risk_id/constraint_id/compat_issue_id/blocker_id/deferred_decision_id), so it is resolved by INV-M2-7's collection-membership check, never here.
validator_contracts.check: resolve each RowRef against step_runs; assert row exists and references the expected upstream step

validator_contracts.rule: unknown-disposition.md — a ground_unknowns_addressed item with disposition='clarification' MUST auto-link to a compatibility issue: its follow_up_ref points at an issue whose requires_user=true, and that issue forces verdict.result=needs_clarification (unless a blocks_flow issue forces blocked first). RECURSES into sub_dispositions[]: a partially_resolved item's residual part with disposition='clarification' applies the SAME auto-link (its follow_up_ref -> requires_user=true issue) — a residual clarification part can never be left un-routed.
validator_contracts.check: for each clarification disposition (top-level AND sub_dispositions[]): assert follow_up_ref -> issue with requires_user=true; assert verdict.result consistent with V2/V3

validator_contracts.rule: unknown-disposition.md — disposition='escalate' MUST link to a blocker issue (blocks_flow=true) forcing verdict.result=blocked; disposition='risk'=>follow_up_ref in risk_surface; disposition='constraint'=>follow_up_ref in constraints; disposition='defer'=>deferred_decisions entry. RECURSES into sub_dispositions[]: a partially_resolved item's residual part with disposition risk/constraint/clarification/defer/escalate applies the SAME disposition->collection mapping to its follow_up_ref (schema if/then already grammar-requires follow_up_ref present for non-resolved sub-dispositions; this check binds it to the correct collection so a residual escalate part links to a real blocker).
validator_contracts.check: assert follow_up_ref target collection matches disposition mapping (risk->risk_surface, constraint->constraints, defer->deferred_decisions, escalate/clarification->issues) for BOTH top-level dispositions AND sub_dispositions[] residual parts

validator_contracts.rule: unknown-disposition.md — matrix_default=false (deviated from recommended disposition) REQUIRES strengthened rationale (non-trivial length / explicit justification). Reviewer parity. OFF-MATRIX unknown_type rule (closes the open-enum hole): unknown_type is a constrained-but-open string, so a value with NO row in the default Matrix (README '기본 Matrix' table) has no recommended disposition — matrix lookup is undefined. In that case matrix_default MUST be false (there is no default to have followed) and the strengthened-rationale requirement applies. matrix_default=true is only legal when unknown_type matches a Matrix row AND disposition equals that row's recommendation.
validator_contracts.check: if unknown_type has no Matrix row assert matrix_default==false; if matrix_default==true assert unknown_type matches a Matrix row AND disposition==that row's recommendation; if matrix_default==false assert rationale length/specificity threshold met

validator_contracts.rule: Reviewer (README L122) — every Ground unknown is addressed (silent 미처리 0). The set of unknown_ref in ground_unknowns_addressed MUST cover every unknown id in the based_on_ground row. PRECONDITION: PC6 has already asserted Ground.unknowns is present and an array, and PC6b has asserted each element has a non-empty id + unknown_type (absent/non-array container => PC6 mechanical error; present-but-malformed element => PC6b mechanical error; neither reaches here) — so this coverage check cannot be vacuously satisfied by a missing/malformed unknowns set nor consume a garbage element id.

compatibility-verdict.md Stale — Decide/Verify compare compatibility_verdict.source_version.ed_snapshot_version against current ED snapshot; mismatch => re-invoke Investigate (cycle cap=1: original + 1 re-invoke = 2 attempts). source_version.ed_snapshot_version MUST equal based_on_ground row's task_subgraph.ed_snapshot_version.
validator_contracts.check: assert source_version.ed_snapshot_version == Ground.task_subgraph.ed_snapshot_version; on later stale, enforce cycle cap

validator_contracts.rule: R16 — declared_next_step (advisory) == orchestrator-injected expected_next_step. Folded into R27 validator body. Mismatch = chain divergence.

R36/provenance — re-hash every sha256/result_hash in verification_proof and source_manifest; re-read source_manifest entries and assert every cited_line <= line_count and exists. ed_queries result_hash re-hashed.
validator_contracts.check: re-hash all *_sha256/result_hash; re-read read_files; assert every source_manifest cited line exists

validator_contracts.rule: R23 — issues_overflow.total_found is NOT a CountClaim (Investigate has no Bash, so no command/raw_stdout): like affected_files_count it is derivation-based. Re-derive total_found.value from total_found.derived_from rows (the enumerated issue rows), NOT from any re-hashed stdout. affected_files_count is the other derivation-based count, covered by INV-M2-1.
validator_contracts.check: assert issues_overflow.total_found.value re-derivable from issues_overflow.total_found.derived_from rows (resolve each RowRef, count the enumerated issue rows); assert value > 50 (overflow precondition)

validator_contracts.rule: README Input preconditions PC5 rule — investigate_error.kind MUST map to which precondition/tool failed: PC1-PC4 + PC6 + PC6b (input-precondition violations) => kind=precondition_violation; PC5 (ED MCP / PRIMARY tool absence/error/timeout) => kind=primary_tool_unavailable (NEVER precondition_violation). This forces the schema if/then to require ed_availability=Omitted on ED failure so an ED-down state is never left un-flagged. failure_origin must agree: PC1/PC4/PC6/PC6b=>ground, PC3=>triage, PC5=>investigate. (PC6: Ground.unknowns absent/non-array => kind=precondition_violation, failure_origin=ground — closes the vacuous-INV-M2-9 hole where an absent unknowns set is silently 'fully covered' by an empty ground_unknowns_addressed. PC6b: each Ground.unknowns[] element MUST have non-empty id + unknown_type => kind=precondition_violation, failure_origin=ground — closes the present-but-malformed-element hole where INV-M2-9 coverage / INV-M2-8 matrix lookup would consume garbage ids/types.) (Cross-field truth — which precondition fired drives kind — grammar cannot see WHICH PC failed, only that kind=primary_tool_unavailable=>ed_availability=Omitted AND evidence pinned to its correct arm (primary_tool_unavailable=>Omitted tool-probe, precondition_violation=>RowRef), both of which the root if/then DOES grammar-enforce — so a precondition_violation can no longer fabricate an Omitted tool-probe evidence.)
validator_contracts.check: assert PC5(ED-down) emitted as kind=primary_tool_unavailable (not precondition_violation); PC1-PC4 + PC6 + PC6b as precondition_violation; failure_origin consistent with the failing PC (ground/triage/investigate)

validator_contracts.rule: external-research.md No-Results table (README 'No-Results 처리') — external_research[].no_results_handling is present IFF result.status=='omitted' (it is only meaningful on a degraded/no-results finding), AND when present its value is the DETERMINISTIC claim_class mapping: decision_critical=>compat_issue, conflict_with_internal=>compat_issue, version_sensitive=>risk_surface, background_context=>defer, feasibility_critical=>negative_signal. A present no_results_handling on a Measured result, an absent one on an Omitted result, or a value that contradicts the claim_class row all FAIL. (Cross-field TRUTH: presence is gated on result.status AND the value is fixed by claim_class — grammar cannot read result.status from the no_results_handling sibling nor key the enum on claim_class.)
validator_contracts.check: for each ExternalFinding: assert (no_results_handling present) == (result.status=='omitted'); when present assert no_results_handling == {decision_critical:'compat_issue', conflict_with_internal:'compat_issue', version_sensitive:'risk_surface', background_context:'defer', feasibility_critical:'negative_signal'}[claim_class]

$comment: Two terminal shapes (README 'Mechanical error 분기', P8/principle 1·3): the NORMAL interpretation output (carries the 5-state compatibility_verdict + impact_map/risk_surface/etc.) OR a MECHANICAL-ERROR output (investigate_error) emitted on input-precondition violation (PC1-PC4+PC6+PC6b) or PRIMARY-tool (ED MCP) failure (PC5) — which produces NO compatibility_verdict and does NOT masquerade as proceed/no_op. The oneOf below is keyed on the presence of investigate_error.

oneOf.$comment: NORMAL output branch — full interpretation. investigate_error MUST be absent. A result-bearing compatibility_verdict can only be produced when ED traversal succeeded, so ed_availability is PINNED to the Measured arm here (README Failure & degrade (1) + reviewer 'Omitted인데 verdict 산출 시 fail'): ED-down (Omitted) can appear ONLY in the mechanical-error branch (kind=primary_tool_unavailable), never alongside a verdict. The Omitted arm is forbidden by requiring the Measured value object.

ed_availability.$comment: Pinned to the Measured arm (value.ed_snapshot_version required) — the Omitted branch (status:'omitted') cannot satisfy this, so ED-down can never carry a normal verdict.

oneOf.$comment: MECHANICAL-ERROR branch — investigate_error REQUIRED; compatibility_verdict and the other interpretation outputs MUST be absent (no empty verdict disguised as proceed/no_op). When kind=primary_tool_unavailable (ED MCP absence/error/timeout), ed_availability MUST be present and pinned to the Omitted branch (README Failure & degrade (1): the PRIMARY-tool failure sets ed_availability=Omitted) — grammar-enforced via the if/then below.

then.$comment: kind=primary_tool_unavailable (ED MCP down) => ed_availability is the ONLY non-identity field allowed in the error branch, and it is REQUIRED + pinned to Omitted (ED-down state never left un-flagged). evidence is ALSO pinned to the Omitted tool-probe arm: a PC5 self-tool failure has NO upstream artifact row, so a RowRef evidence is forbidden here (README L318: the Omitted tool-probe arm is reserved for PC5).

ed_availability.$comment: PRIMARY-tool failure => ED unusable => ed_availability MUST be the Omitted branch (not Measured/absent). Reuses the shared Omitted $def.

evidence.$comment: PC5 tool-probe arm only — pinned to Omitted (no upstream RowRef exists for a self-tool failure).

else.$comment: kind=precondition_violation (PC1-PC4 + PC6 + PC6b) => the error shape carries EXACTLY {kind, failure_origin, reason, evidence} + root identity; ed_availability is a non-identity interpretation field excluded from the error shape (README L318/L321 'precondition_violation에는 불요'), so it is forbidden here. evidence is pinned to the RowRef arm: a precondition violation MUST point at the offending Ground/Triage artifact row, never fabricate an Omitted tool-probe (README L318).

evidence.$comment: PC1-PC4 + PC6 + PC6b arm only — pinned to the RowRef of the failing upstream artifact row (no tool-probe Omitted allowed).

flow_id.description: The flow this investigation belongs to. §5 RowRef (flows.id), never a path.

based_on.description: §5 BASED-ON bundle — upstream rows this investigation consumes (Ground + Triage). All values RowRef. Investigate REQUIRES ground_ref + triage_ref (its two declared inputs). The shared BasedOn $def is the single source for every key→RowRef mapping (M4); the two required keys are re-stated locally only to pin `required` (RowRef type unchanged).

based_on_ground.description: The specific Ground step_run row this investigation derives from (legacy 'based_on_ground: <ground 산출물 hash>' translated to §5 RowRef). M2 resolves it to re-derive affected_files, validate unknown coverage, and check ed_snapshot_version freshness.

depth.description: Adaptive Step Depth this Investigate ran at (shallow=compatibility+unknown_disposition only; deep=full 6 activities). Deepen triggers in README L132-137.

impact_map.description: Impact tracing from Ground entry_nodes (ED traversal — callers/callees/data flow). R6 INPUT for Decide. affected_files / affected_files_count are mechanically derived (M2 INV-M2-1/2).

primary_areas.description: Directly-impacted nodes. source is the ED traversal that produced each (provenance).

source.description: How this primary area was identified — legacy 'source: ed_traversal'. Provenance (SourceTool).

ripple.description: Indirect/transitive impact — nodes reached at a graph distance from primary areas, with reason.

distance.description: Graph hop distance from primary area. A small bounded traversal-depth integer (NOT a measured count of tool output) — not a CountClaim.

external_surface.description: Public contracts crossing the change boundary. breaking=true entries feed architecture_impact.public_api_changes (M2 INV-M2-3).

contract.description: The public symbol/contract crossing the boundary.

breaking.description: Whether the change to this contract is breaking. true => contributes to public_api_changes + has_architecture_level (M2 derived).

affected_files.description: Mechanically derived union of Ground.task_subgraph.entry_nodes file paths + ripple file-type node paths, dedup'd (README L77). These are READ-INPUT path strings (real repo files the impact touches), NOT §5 cross-step refs — file paths are correct here.

affected_files_count.description: R23 — count of affected files = affected_files.length (R6 mechanical-eval cache). NOT a CountClaim: Investigate has NO Bash (README 'Bash 도구 제거') so there is no shell command + raw_stdout that produces this count. It is DERIVED from upstream structured graph rows (Ground.task_subgraph.entry_nodes + impact_map.ripple), so it carries DERIVATION provenance (derived_from RowRefs + source_tool) instead of CountClaim's command+raw_stdout. M2 INV-M2-1 re-derives value == dedup(affected_files).length from those rows; a bare integer is still the R23 hallucination.

value.description: The counted integer. Meaningless without derived_from — never emit alone (R23 polarity).

derived_from.description: §5 RowRefs of the upstream structured rows this count is mechanically derived from (the based_on_ground row's task_subgraph + this investigation's ripple). M2 INV-M2-1 re-derives value from these, not from any shell stdout.

source_tool.description: R26 provenance — the ED traversal / graph derivation that produced this count (no shell command exists for Investigate).

constraints.description: Constraints derived from policy/contract/security posture/domain. blocking=true marks flow-blocking constraints. Each carries source_tool provenance (R26).

blocking.description: Optional — whether absence/violation of this constraint blocks the flow.

constraint_id.description: Optional invocation-scoped id so a ground_unknowns_addressed disposition='constraint' can target it via follow_up_ref.

source_tool.description: R26 provenance — what produced this constraint (rule file read, contract surface, security probe).

risk_surface.description: Failure modes = impact × Ground concerns. Each item: area + severity (RISK scale low|med|high|critical) + probability + evidence + source_tool. Reviewer requires risk_surface to cover the high-impact nodes of impact_map (primary_areas/ripple), derived in Activity 3 — no reference to any undefined input.

severity.description: RISK scale (low|med|high|critical) — distinct from the compatibility-issue scale (fatal|high|medium|low). Do not collapse.

evidence.description: Evidence backing this risk. Free-text justification of the failure mode.

evidence_ref.description: Optional §5 RowRef to the Ground/Investigate fact row that grounds this risk (provenance pointer).

risk_id.description: Optional invocation-scoped id so a ground_unknowns_addressed disposition='risk' can target it via follow_up_ref.

source_tool.description: R26 provenance — what surfaced this risk.

architecture_impact.description: R6 force INPUT (Decide reads). Investigate identifies AFFECTED new modules / changed public API during impact analysis — it does NOT generate or select options (Decide). has_architecture_level is orchestrator-DERIVED (M2 INV-M2-3), not free-filled here, but carried for the grammar shape.

new_modules.description: New directory paths / module symbols surfaced by ED traversal + ripple (impact identification only, NOT option generation). Path/symbol strings.

public_api_changes.description: Public symbols/contracts changed = external_surface[] entries with breaking==true (M2 INV-M2-3 re-derives).

has_architecture_level.description: R6 force-trigger = (new_modules.length>0) OR (public_api_changes.length>0). Orchestrator-derived (README L80); carried in the shape, validated by M2 INV-M2-3.

compatibility_verdict.description: The 5-state verdict (compatibility-verdict.md). DISCRIMINATED UNION on `result`: proceed | blocked | needs_clarification | no_op | partial_proceed. Per-branch required fields enforce V8/V9/V11/V13 SHAPE at grammar level; the value-truth of V1-V7/V10/V12 is M2 (INV-M2-4). source_version freshness is M2 (INV-M2-10).

result.description: 5-state compatibility result. Discriminant for the oneOf below.

checked_at.description: When this verdict was computed (freshness; Verify race-detects against current time).

source_version.description: Freshness anchors. ed_snapshot_version MUST equal based_on_ground row's task_subgraph.ed_snapshot_version (M2 INV-M2-10 stale detection, cycle cap=1).

issues.description: Scoped compatibility issues. cap 50 (V6 — over-cap requires issues_overflow), dedup most-severe-wins (V5). Each issue's flags drive the result (V2 blocks_flow=>blocked, V3 requires_user=>needs_clarification) — that derivation is M2 (INV-M2-4).

reason.description: Why this result was reached (V-rule narrative).

sub_flow_verdicts.description: Compound only — per-sub-flow verdict. issue_refs are issue ids within this verdict.

issues_overflow.description: V6 — present iff total_found > 50. total_found is NOT a CountClaim: Investigate has NO Bash, so there is no shell command + raw_stdout that enumerates the issue set. The issue set is enumerated via ED traversal / LLM judgment over the upstream graph rows, so total_found carries DERIVATION provenance (value + derived_from RowRefs + source_tool), the same shape as impact_map.affected_files_count.

total_found.description: R23 — total issues found before the 50-cap. NOT a CountClaim (Investigate has no Bash; the issue set is enumerated, not produced by a shell command + raw_stdout). DERIVED from the enumerated issue rows: value + derived_from RowRefs + source_tool. M2 INV-M2-13 re-derives value from those rows.

value.description: The total issue count before the cap. Meaningless without derived_from — never emit alone (R23 polarity).

derived_from.description: §5 RowRefs of the enumerated issue rows this count is derived from. M2 INV-M2-13 re-derives value from these, not from any shell stdout.

source_tool.description: R26 provenance — the ED traversal / enumeration that produced this count (no shell command exists for Investigate).

captured.description: Number captured (always 50 at overflow). Fixed cap constant, not a measured-from-tool count.

oneOf.$comment: V1/V4 — empty issues OR no blocking/clarification flags => proceed. blockers/open_questions/no_op_details/partial_scope_handling MUST be absent.
V2/V8 — any issue.blocks_flow=true => blocked. blockers (issue ids) REQUIRED non-empty.

blockers.description: V8 — issue ids that block the flow. REQUIRED non-empty when result=blocked.

oneOf.$comment: V3/V9 — (no blocks_flow) any issue.requires_user=true => needs_clarification. open_questions (issue ids) REQUIRED non-empty.

open_questions.description: V9 — issue ids requiring user/caller answer. REQUIRED non-empty when result=needs_clarification.

no_op_details.description: V11 — required no-op justification. evidence is a §5 RowRef to the Ground/Investigate fact comparison.

target_state.description: Triage-intended target extracted from the request.

partial_scope_handling.description: V13 — scope split. proceed_set/blocked_set are scope refs (issue ids / scope identifiers); blocked_set is queued as followup_flows by Decide.

no_op_set.description: Compound mixed only (V14b) — sub_flow_ids whose result=no_op (legitimately-empty; NOT blocked_set/proceed_set, Decide does not process them).

ground_unknowns_addressed.description: The 7-disposition matrix (unknown-disposition.md). EVERY Ground unknown MUST appear exactly once (silent 미처리 0 — M2 INV-M2-9 coverage). disposition enum + rationale REQUIRED on each; follow_up_ref + matrix_default + sub_dispositions per disposition (M2 auto-link checks INV-M2-6/7/8).

deferred_decisions.description: Records for disposition='defer' items — what is punted to a later step. follow_up_ref of a deferred unknown targets a deferred_decision_id here (M2 INV-M2-7).

defer_to.description: Which later step resolves this (defer_to: decide | spec | test | implement).

sub_flow_identification.description: Compound only — identifies sub-flows (flow_type + scope + rationale). IDENTIFICATION only; decomposition/ordering is Decide (boundary). source_tool provenance per item.

triage_mismatch.description: Optional — surfaced only when Triage misclassification is suspected (reclassify trigger). suspected_flow_type + evidence. source_tool provenance.

external_research.description: Optional — external-research.md findings (WebFetch/WebSearch/Context7) used as INTERPRETATION AID (not project-internal capture). Each finding is DEGRADABLE: an external source can be inaccessible/rate-limited/paywalled/auth-required — those are Omitted branches (R22), not nulls. claim_class drives provenance depth.

verification_proof.description: Provenance container backing this investigation's facts: tool_calls + read_files + optional ed_queries (ED is MCP-backed; when ED is unavailable the ED-derived field uses Omitted, not an empty ed_queries). M2 INV-M2-12 re-hashes all.

source_manifest.description: Optional R36 — files cited (CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/.claude/rules/** etc.) with exact cited_lines. M2 INV-M2-12 asserts every cited line exists.

ed_availability.description: M3 — overall ED/MCP graph availability for this run. ED traversal (Activity 1) is the core measured surface and can be ABSENT/timeout; this is the first-class degrade branch. Measured.value carries the snapshot version queried; Omitted carries why ED was unusable (=> impact_map traversal degrades, unknowns lean to escalate per matrix).

declared_next_step.description: R16 — advisory declared successor. Orchestrator INJECTS the authoritative expected_next_step; M2 INV-M2-11 asserts equality. For a proceed/partial_proceed verdict the chain typically continues to 'decide'.

unverified.description: R13 floor field — KEEP polarity. Marks investigation claims the step could not itself verify; DESIGNED to persist downstream until Verify (the single gate). NO 'no unverified remain' forbid exists.

investigate_error.description: MECHANICAL-ERROR terminal output (README 'Failure & degrade handling', P8/principle 1·3). Emitted on input-precondition violation (PC1-PC4 + PC6 + PC6b) or PRIMARY-tool (ED MCP) absence/error/timeout (PC5) — OUTSIDE the 5-state compatibility_verdict. When present, NO compatibility_verdict is produced (the root oneOf forbids it), so a missing/degenerate input is never disguised as proceed/no_op.

kind.description: Which class of mechanical error terminated the step.

failure_origin.description: Which upstream/self origin the error is attributed to. Subset of the shared FailureOrigin enum used by Investigate's mechanical-error escalate paths: ground/triage on input preconditions, investigate on its own PRIMARY tool. (README keeps this to 3 values; cap_exceeded halts are an orchestrator-level cap concern, not an investigate_error origin.)

evidence.description: What evidences the failure. oneOf: (a) a §5 RowRef to the Ground/Triage artifact row whose missing/malformed field tripped a PC1-PC4 + PC6 + PC6b input precondition (kind=precondition_violation); OR (b) a TOOL-PROBE provenance object for a PC5 self-tool failure (kind=primary_tool_unavailable) — ED MCP absence/timeout has NO upstream artifact row, so it cannot be a RowRef. The probe object reuses the shared Omitted $def (the same absence cause/source_tool grammar used for ed_availability), keeping a self-tool failure from being forced to fabricate a RowRef.

oneOf.$comment: PC1-PC4 + PC6 + PC6b input-precondition violations: the failing upstream artifact row.
PC5 primary-tool failure: tool-probe result (no upstream row). Reuses Omitted (status:'omitted' + reason + source_tool) — same shape as ed_availability's Omitted branch.

CompatibilityIssue.$comment: compatibility-verdict.md issue schema. type enum (15 + other), severity=SeverityIssue (fatal|high|medium|low — NOT the risk scale), evidence REQUIRED (V10 provenance RowRef), flags drive V2/V3 result (M2).

id.description: Invocation-scoped unique issue id (referenced by blockers/open_questions/proceed_set/blocked_set/sub_flow_verdicts.issue_refs and unknown follow_up_ref).

type.description: Issue type enum. type='other' REQUIRES custom_type (V7).

custom_type.description: REQUIRED when type='other' (V7 — enforced by the if/then below).

severity.description: COMPATIBILITY-ISSUE scale fatal|high|medium|low (SeverityIssue) — distinct from risk_surface Severity (low|med|high|critical). Do not collapse.

scope.description: All sub-fields optional; all-empty = project-wide. target_set is bounded (top N + summary).

target_set.description: Bounded set of affected consumer ids (top N + summary).

evidence.description: V10 — REQUIRED provenance. §5 RowRef to the Ground/Investigate fact backing this issue (legacy '<ground/investigate ref>'). M2 resolves the row.

requires_user.description: true => contributes to needs_clarification (V3). M2 INV-M2-4 derives the result.

blocks_flow.description: true => contributes to blocked (V2). M2 INV-M2-4 derives the result.

scope_confined.description: Only meaningful when blocks_flow=true. true => the block is confined to a bounded scope (proceed remainder exists => partial_proceed candidate, V2b); false/absent => flow-wide block (=> blocked, V2a). Mechanically derived per V2/V13 — the load-bearing V2a/V2b discriminant the reviewer checks.

source_tool.description: R26 provenance — what tool/origin surfaced this issue (in addition to the evidence RowRef).

UnknownDisposition.$comment: unknown-disposition.md — one Ground unknown -> exactly one of 7 dispositions. disposition+rationale REQUIRED. partially_resolved REQUIRES sub_dispositions (if/then). source_tool provenance on the disposition item.

unknown_ref.description: §5 RowRef (or sub-row id) of the Ground unknown being disposed. M2 INV-M2-9 asserts coverage of every Ground unknown.

unknown_type.description: Ground unknown type for matrix matching (capture_failed:timeout, capture_failed:tool_error, inaccessible:permission_denied, tool_unavailable, referent_unresolved, ed_ambiguous, ed_inferred, ed_drift, external_unknown, contradiction, racing_changes, ...). Kept as a constrained-but-open string to stay faithful to the matrix without inventing a closed enum beyond the prose. OFF-MATRIX rule (INV-M2-8): a value with no Matrix row has no recommended disposition, so matrix_default MUST be false (strengthened rationale required) — matrix_default=true is only legal for a type matching a Matrix row with the recommended disposition.

disposition.description: One of the 7 dispositions. clarification auto-links to a requires_user issue (M2 INV-M2-6); escalate to a blocks_flow issue (M2 INV-M2-7); risk->risk_surface; constraint->constraints; defer->deferred_decisions.

rationale.description: Why this disposition. matrix_default=false REQUIRES strengthened rationale (M2 INV-M2-8).

matrix_default.description: Optional — true if this followed the matrix-recommended disposition (only legal when unknown_type matches a Matrix row AND disposition==that row's recommendation). false => rationale must be strengthened (M2 INV-M2-8). An OFF-MATRIX unknown_type (no Matrix row) FORCES false (no default to follow).

follow_up_ref.description: Optional LOCAL invocation-scoped id (NOT a §5 RowRef) of the in-document collection item this disposition produced: risk_id (risk_surface[]) | constraint_id (constraints[]) | compat_issue_id/blocker_id (compatibility_verdict.issues[].id) | deferred_decision_id (deferred_decisions[]). All five targets are invocation-scoped string ids living inside THIS output, not Postgres step_runs rows — so this is a plain local id string, not a RowRef (it is NOT resolved by INV-M2-5; collection-membership is checked by INV-M2-7). REQUIRED in practice for risk/constraint/clarification/defer/escalate (M2 enforces the disposition->collection mapping; resolved/partially_resolved-fully may omit).

sub_dispositions.description: REQUIRED when disposition='partially_resolved' (if/then) — each residual part gets its own disposition + follow_up_ref.

follow_up_ref.description: LOCAL invocation-scoped id (NOT a §5 RowRef) of the in-document collection item this residual part produced (risk_id | constraint_id | compat_issue_id/blocker_id | deferred_decision_id), same target space as the top-level follow_up_ref. REQUIRED for any non-resolved sub-disposition (risk/constraint/clarification/defer/escalate) — the if/then below grammar-enforces presence so a residual escalate/clarification part can never be left un-routed (same no-silent-미처리 rule as top-level). The disposition->collection MAPPING (escalate->blocker issue, clarification->requires_user issue, risk->risk_surface, constraint->constraints, defer->deferred_decisions) is cross-field TRUTH, recursed by M2 INV-M2-7 (collection-membership; NOT INV-M2-5 RowRef-resolution).

source_tool.description: R26 provenance — how this unknown was investigated/disposed (e.g. WebFetch for resolved, ED query, matrix-default).

ExternalFinding.$comment: external-research.md — a single external claim resolution. DEGRADABLE: resolved at a trust grade (Measured) OR inaccessible/rate-limited/paywall/auth/no-results (Omitted, R22). claim_class drives provenance depth.

claim_class.description: external-research.md provenance-depth driver. decision_critical/version_sensitive/conflict_with_internal/feasibility_critical => full provenance; background_context => aggregated.

no_results_handling.description: Optional sibling of `result` (only meaningful when result is Omitted) — external-research.md no-results disposition by claim_class (covers ALL 5 claim_class values so no Omitted+claim_class combination is silent): decision_critical=>compat_issue; conflict_with_internal=>compat_issue (external check failed => unresolved internal-vs-external conflict blocks / needs user); version_sensitive=>risk_surface; background_context=>defer; feasibility_critical=>negative_signal. Lifted out of the Omitted branch because the shared Omitted $def is additionalProperties:false (cannot be extended in place).

result.description: M3 DEGRADE — Measured (claim verified at a trust grade) | Omitted (external source inaccessible / rate-limited / paywall / auth-required / no-results). Absence is a first-class branch (R22), never null. The shared Omitted $def is used as-is (no in-place extension).

trust.description: Source trust grade (external-research.md). generated_seo is never authoritative.

provenance.description: Provenance depth scales with claim_class. decision_critical/version_sensitive/conflict/feasibility_critical => full (url+accessed_at+content_hash+source_type+version_snapshot); background => aggregated (sources_consulted+primary).
